title: Common DISC1 Polymorphisms Disrupt Wnt/GSK3beta Signaling and Brain Development
claim_summary: Common DISC1 gene polymorphisms disrupt Wnt/GSK3beta signaling pathways and impair cortical neuron development, suggesting a molecular mechanism linking DISC1 variants to psychiatric disorder risk.
stated_reason: Correction of duplication errors in figures; per the notice text the conclusions of the paper have not been impacted. Issued after a seven-year delay following a PubPeer exchange.
selection_method: Search: Retraction Watch Neuron correction 2025. Surfaced via RW post 2025-05-20. Low fame outside PubPeer community. Selected as a terse-end correction; claim_stands_after_event confirmed publisher-stated via notice (PMC 40318629): figure-specific, conclusions explicitly unaffected.
verification_note: cursor 2026-06-12: original DOI corrected from 2018.09.014 (different Neuron paper, alpha-synuclein tetramers) to 2011.09.030; notice DOI 2025.04.021 added; terseness terse->mixed (notice is figure-specific, not a one-liner); claim_stands upgraded from inferred to publisher-stated per PMC notice text.
corpus_scope: DISC1 psychiatric genetics, delayed corrections in Cell Press journals
